title: Find maximum in an Array

content.problemStatement:
Given a list of NNN integers, representing height of mountains. Find the height of the tallest mountain.

Input:
First line will contain TTT, number of testcases. Then the testcases follow.
The first line in each testcase contains one integer, NNN.
The following line contains NNN space separated integers: the height of each mountains.
Output:For each testcase, output one line with one integer: the height of the tallest mountain for that test case.
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains an integer N, the number of mountains; the second line contains N space‑separated integers denoting the heights of the mountains.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing one integer – the maximum height among the given mountains.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The heights are [4,7,6,3,1]; the largest value is 7.
Only one mountain exists with height 10^9, so that is the answer.

solution.approach: The tallest mountain is simply the maximum element of the array. Scan the N heights once while keeping the current maximum. This linear scan works because any element larger than the current maximum becomes the new answer, and after processing all elements the stored value is the overall maximum. The algorithm is optimal: any solution must look at each element at least once, giving O(N) time per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(total N) over all test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra space besides input storage.

code.Java: import java.io.*;import java.util.*;public class Main{public static void main(String[] args) throws Exception{BufferedReader br=new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(System.in));StringBuilder sb=new StringBuilder();int T=Integer.parseInt(br.readLine().trim());while(T-- >0){int N=Integer.parseInt(br.readLine().trim());StringTokenizer st=new StringTokenizer(br.readLine());long max=Long.MIN_VALUE;for(int i=0;i<N;i++){long h=Long.parseLong(st.nextToken());if(h>max)max=h;}sb.append(max).append('\n');}System.out.print(sb.toString());}}
